# Reliability Checklist: Optional gh-stack stack manager integration

**Purpose**: Validate reliability requirement quality for deterministic command plans, evidence paths, stack topology preservation, fake-CLI coverage, Layer 7 replay scope, and blocked stack-manager resume behavior.
**Created**: 2026-06-14
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are stack-manager command plans defined as ordered argv-array records with stable action, manager, mutation-boundary, slice, review-order, precondition, and reason fields? [Resolved, Spec §FR-019, Data Model §Command Plan, Contract §command_plan_item]
- [x] CHK002 Are stack-manager decision, read-only proof, command execution, recovery, and workflow evidence paths required to be deterministic, repo-relative `.process` paths that can be reopened on resume? [Resolved, Spec §FR-024, Data Model §Stack-Manager Evidence Path, Contract §evidence_path]
- [x] CHK003 Are stdout/stderr evidence bounds defined so failed or ambiguous CLI output cannot create unbounded artifacts? [Resolved, Spec §FR-021, Plan §Technical Context, Research §Decision 7]
- [x] CHK004 Are stack-manager command plans forbidden from storing joined shell strings as executable state? [Resolved, Spec §FR-019, Data Model §Command Plan]

## Topology Preservation

- [x] CHK005 Are PRSG-013 marker order, branch names, and explicit base topology required to remain unchanged across both `gh-stack` and explicit fallback paths? [Resolved, Spec §FR-007, Quickstart §3, Data Model §Topology Evidence]
- [x] CHK006 Does topology compatibility compare expected PRS/marker topology with observed `gh stack view --json` topology before mutation? [Resolved, Spec §Assumptions, Research §Decision 2, Data Model §Read-Only Proof]
- [x] CHK007 Are branch and base relationships tied to `review_order`, slice identity, head SHA, PR number/URL, and packet identity for retry reconciliation? [Resolved, Spec §FR-014, Data Model §Topology Evidence, Quickstart §6]

## Resume Reliability

- [x] CHK008 Does blocked stack-manager resume require a preflight that reloads prior recovery evidence, supersedes stale blocked workflow events deterministically, and proves same-manager no-change reconciliation before any resumed create/sync/restack command? [Resolved, Spec §FR-025, Data Model §Recoverable Block State, Quickstart §11, Contract §recovery]
- [x] CHK009 Are partial and unknown mutating `gh-stack` failures forbidden from switching to explicit `gh` unless same-manager reconciliation proves no topology change? [Resolved, Spec §FR-011, Plan §Error classification rules, Contract §selected_manager=blocked]
- [x] CHK010 Are duplicate PR retry checks required before any create, sync, or manager switch? [Resolved, Spec §FR-014, Quickstart §6]

## Fixture and Replay Coverage

- [x] CHK011 Are Layer 4 fake-CLI fixtures the authoritative proof for support selection, fallback, mutation boundary, retry reconciliation, and restack behavior? [Resolved, Spec §Verification Scope, Quickstart §Validation Scenarios]
- [x] CHK012 Do runtime fixtures fake canonical `gh stack` through fake `gh` argv dispatch instead of requiring real `gh`, real `gh stack`, or networked GitHub state? [Resolved, Spec §Verification Scope, Plan §Testing]
- [x] CHK013 Are Layer 7 PRSG-014 replay expectations limited to orchestration shape, no `grill-me`, and operator-facing stack-manager evidence terms? [Resolved, Spec §FR-023, Quickstart §9, Workflow §Checklist Prompts]
- [x] CHK014 Are Layer 8 expectations scoped to Claude/Codex operator guidance parity rather than transcript-level executable behavior? [Resolved, Spec §Verification Scope, Quickstart §10]

## Notes

- Initial reliability pass found two gaps: deterministic stack-manager evidence path policy and explicit blocked-resume superseding/preflight behavior.
- Resolved CHK002 by adding FR-024, a Stack-Manager Evidence Path entity, data-model path families, plan reliability rules, and schema path constraints for repo-relative `.process` evidence.
- Resolved CHK008 by adding FR-025, blocked resume preflight fields (`blocked_event_id`, `resume_preflight`, `stale_result_policy`) to the recovery schema/data model, and Quickstart scenario 11.

## Verification Rerun

- [x] CHK015 Does the stack-manager decision contract now constrain persisted evidence paths to repo-relative feature `.process` or workflow `.process` paths? [Resolved Verification, Contract §evidence_path]
- [x] CHK016 Does blocked resume now require reload/revalidate/supersede behavior before any resumed create, sync, restack, or manager switch? [Resolved Verification, Spec §FR-025, Quickstart §11]
